frameworks.name: contralateral delay activity (CDA)
frameworks.one_line: ERP marker scaling with the number of items held in visual working memory

frameworks.name: storage-load account of CDA
frameworks.one_line: CDA indexes total contents of working memory across displays

frameworks.name: attentional-focus account of CDA
frameworks.one_line: CDA tracks the current focus of spatial attention rather than total storage

core_question: Does contralateral delay activity index total working-memory storage load or only the current focus of spatial attention?
key_assumptions: CDA amplitude reliably scales with stored item count
Sequentially presented arrays in opposite hemifields tax both lateralized memory stores
Probe-sample spatial correspondence modulates whether earlier items are offloaded
manipulations: Spatially compatible vs incompatible sample-probe arrays
Sequential presentation of memoranda across opposite hemifields
Replication of a prior incompatible-probe paradigm
key_findings: With spatially compatible probes, CDA reflected total storage load across both arrays
With spatially incompatible probes, CDA was dominated by the second array, replicating prior work
interpretations: CDA tracks the total contents of working memory, not just the current attentional focus
Apparent attention-only effects arise when probe mismatch encourages offloading the first array
